Wrapper functions simplify writing computer programs by abstracting the details of a subroutine's implementation. Purpose [ edit ] Wrapper functions a | Purpose [ edit ] 
 Wrapper functions are a means of delegation and can be used for a number of purposes. | A wrapper can be written for each of the third party functions and used in the native application. | Adapting class/object interfaces [ edit ] 
 
 Wrapper functions can be used to adapt an existing class or object to have a different interface. | Code testing [ edit ] 
 Wrapper functions can be used to write error checking routines for pre-existing system functions without increasing the length
